findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.pass-body-fn
findings.description: `pass` is the body of function get_current_user. Consider removing this or raise NotImplementedError() if this is a TODO

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.jwt.security.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.correctness.useless-assignment
findings.description: `document.cookie` is assigned twice; the first assignment is useless
findings.code_excerpt:
document.cookie = "username=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 UTC; path=/";
        document.cookie = "passwordHash=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 UTC; path=/";

tool_specific.lines:
document.cookie = "username=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 UTC; path=/";
        document.cookie = "passwordHash=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 UTC; path=/";

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'run' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.deserialization.avoid-pickle

owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.boto3.security.hardcoded-token
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.requests.best-practice.use-timeout
findings.description: Detected a 'requests' call without a timeout set. By default, 'requests' calls wait until the connection is closed. This means a 'requests' call without a timeout will hang the program if a response is never received. Consider setting a timeout for all 'requests'.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string
findings.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse
findings.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-fs-filename
findings.description: Detected that function argument `req` has entered the fs module. An attacker could potentially control the location of this file, to include going backwards in the directory with '../'. To address this, ensure that user-controlled variables in file paths are validated.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
